title: Allow inline editing of Status and Priority in the bead table
description: Add inline dropdowns directly in the Status and Priority columns of the bead list table. Clicking a cell opens a select without needing to open the detail drawer. Calls bd update on change.
close_reason: Implemented: inline MUI Select for Status and Priority in BeadList table with optimistic updates

title: Change status filter to multi-select, default to all except closed and deferred
description: Replace the single-value Status dropdown in the bead table filter bar with a multi-select. Default selection: open, in_progress, blocked, pinned. Empty selection shows all beads.

title: Add Docker healthchecks to docker-compose.yml
description: Add HEALTHCHECK instructions so Docker Desktop shows container health status and the frontend container waits for the backend to be ready before starting. Backend check: GET /health. Frontend check: nginx -t or curl localhost.

title: Add Joi input validation to backend routes
description: The POST /api/projects and POST /api/projects/:id/beads routes accept unvalidated payloads. Add @hapi/joi validation schemas to reject malformed input early and return 400 with clear messages instead of letting errors bubble up from bd.

title: Cross-project search
description: Add a global search bar on the home page that queries bd list across all registered projects and shows results grouped by project. Useful when you remember a bead title but not which project it belongs to.
close_reason: Removed from backlog — out of scope for now

title: Dashboard with bead counts by status and priority
description: Add a summary dashboard on the project page showing bead counts grouped by status (open, in_progress, blocked, closed) and by priority. Gives an at-a-glance health view of the project.

title: bd list omits closed beads in the web UI
description: The backend calls 'bd list --json' without --all, so closed beads are never returned. The web UI should show all beads and let the user filter.
close_reason: Fixed: bd list now called with --all --limit 0

title: Bead list shows blank lines instead of bead data
description: The project page loads successfully and shows rows, but all cells are empty. The bd list --json output format likely doesn't match the field names the frontend expects.
close_reason: Fixed: all PascalCase field refs updated to snake_case to match bd JSON output; getBead now unwraps the array from bd show

title: Internal server error when opening a project page
description: Clicking a project tile shows 'An internal server error occurred'. Root cause: bd CLI is not available inside the Docker container, and Hapi masks 5xx error messages so the real reason is hidden.
close_reason: Fixed: install @beads/bd via npm in Dockerfile; replace Boom.badImplementation with Boom.serverUnavailable

title: Persist bead list scroll position and filters across navigation
description: When a user opens a bead detail drawer and closes it, or navigates back from a sub-page, the status/type filters and scroll position reset. Persist filter state in URL search params (?status=open&type=bug) so the browser back button restores context.

title: Remove obsolete version field from docker-compose.yml
description: docker-compose.yml has 'version: 3.8' which is now obsolete in Docker Compose v2 and produces a warning on every startup. Remove the field.

title: Auto-refresh bead list when project files change
description: Poll or use fs.watch on the project's .beads/issues.jsonl file from the backend and push updates to the frontend via Server-Sent Events (SSE). This way the list stays fresh when bd is used from the CLI in parallel.

title: Bead dependency graph view
description: Visualize bead dependencies using bd graph output. Show a DAG (directed acyclic graph) of blocking/blocked-by relationships on the project page. Could use a lightweight library like d3-dag or react-flow.

title: Comments on beads
description: Surface bd comments <id> output in the bead detail drawer. Allow adding new comments via the web UI using bd comment add (if supported). Comments are already stored in the Dolt database.
